title: miyamayomena savatieri subsp. pygmaea
intro: Miyamayomena savatieri subsp. pygmaea, also known as the pygmy Miyamayomena, is a rare and endemic plant species found in New Caledonia. It belongs to the family Gesneriaceae and is a small, herbaceous perennial plant. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides. The health benefits of Miyamayomena savatieri subsp. pygmaea include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and it can be prepared as an herbal infusion or decoction.
